candidate.text: At the elevator lobby on Tier 1, it was noticed that during heavy rain and wind events, water starts to accumulate under the stair landings, traveling through the North access of the tower. With the high wind forces, the water starts to flow towards the elevator fronts. This causes the water to start flowing into the elevator pits. All the slopes have been installed per the drawings and verified in the field, per heat map included in RFI attachment. Per meeting on 3.14 with DES, Broadway, DPR, and PMA, it is proposed to install a trench drain parallel with the elevator fronts (see attached markup) with perpendicular legs at either end. Drain would feed back into the hub drain located at the fire riser. This method aligns with the walk with ownership on 3.4.25. 1. DES - Please confirm extent of drain shown in the attached markup is acceptable. If not, please define desired extents. 2. DES - Please confirm the trench drain product attached is acceptable. Please confirm grate finish selection. 3. GPLA - Please note that demo of elevator lobby SOG will be necessary as part of this RFI. Please provide structural considerations necessary for this demo and pour back details.
candidate.resolution: Julia Morini (DES Architects & Engineers, Inc. - 10012655): Please see attached.

highly_relevant.reasoning: The historical issue describes a similar problem of water accumulation at the elevator lobby during heavy rain, and the proposed solution of installing a trench drain directly addresses the new observation.

- PSD Tier 1 Elevator Lobby Trench Drain - Water Remediation RFI detail #2046.1 PSD Tier 1 Elevator Lobby Trench Drain - Water  Remediation

At the elevator lobby on Tier 1, it was noticed that during heavy rain and wind events, water starts to accumulate 
under the stair landings, traveling through the North access of the tower. With the high wind forces, the water 
starts to flow towards the elevator fronts. This causes the water to start flowing into the elevator pits. All the 
slopes have been installed per the drawings and verified in the field, per heat map included in RFI attachment.
Per meeting on 3.14 with DES, Broadway, DPR, and PMA, it is proposed to install a trench drain parallel with the 
elevator fronts (see attached markup) with perpendicular legs at either end. Drain would feed back into the hub 
drain located at the fire riser. This method aligns with the walk with ownership on 3.4.25.
1. DES - Please confirm extent of drain shown in the attached markup is acceptable. If not, please define desired 
extents.
2. DES - Please confirm the trench drain product attached is acceptable. Please confirm grate finish selection.
3. GPLA - Please note that demo of elevator lobby SOG will be necessary as part of this RFI. Please provide 
structural considerations necessary for this demo and pour back details.

vehicular cable rail will be needed on the ramp side of GB between G13 and G14 where a slab opening occurs.

tier 5 slab and there are PT tendons terminate within the center of the G13 column. 
Question 1: Please confirm it is acceptable to use a post installed anchor in lieu of an embed at the location 
shown.

within column. This embed would be installed after the shear wall is poured.
